text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY -- FOLLOW-UP VISIT NOTE
Hematology/Oncology | Thoracic Malignancies Program

ATTENDING: [Attending Oncologist]
FELLOW: [Fellow]
PATIENT: Margaret Yuen
DOB: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
Visit Type: Established Patient Follow-Up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Follow-up for Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant pulmonary adenocarcinoma, on osi (Tagrisso) monotherapy. Patient presents approximately 6 months after initiation of systemic therapy for routine reassessment and review of restaging imaging.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Yuen is a 62-year-old Chinese-American woman, retired high school chemistry teacher, with a history of Stage IIIB (T3N2M0) non-small cell lung cancer, acinar-predominant pulmonary adenocarcinoma of the right upper lobe, harboring an EGFR exon 21 L858R sensitizing mutation. She has a 20 pack-year smoking history and quit approximately 12 years prior to her cancer diagnosis. Relevant comorbidities include well-controlled type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypertension, and mild COPD (GOLD Stage I).

She was diagnosed approximately 6 months ago following evaluation for progressive exertional dyspnea and a persistent productive cough. Initial staging workup demonstrated a 4.8 cm spiculated right upper lobe mass with hypermetabolic bilateral mediastinal lymphadenopathy at stations 4R and 7 (SUVmax 8.7 and 6.4, respectively) on PET-CT, without evidence of distant metastatic disease. Brain MRI at staging was negative for intracranial metastases. CT-guided biopsy of the primary mass confirmed invasive adenocarcinoma (TTF-1+, Napsin-A+, acinar-predominant), with concurrent EBUS-guided FNA of station 4R confirming nodal metastatic disease. Molecular profiling by NGS revealed EGFR L858R (VAF 42%), co-mutations in TP53 R248W (VAF 38%), and a low-level CDK6 copy number gain. No ALK, ROS1, or other targetable fusions were identified. PD-L1 TPS was 15% by 22C3. TMB was 6.2 mut/Mb (low). MSI-stable.

The patient was evaluated by thoracic surgery (felt unresectable given multi-station N2 nodal involvement) and radiation oncology. At multidisciplinary tumor board, the team discussed the standard-of-care approach of concurrent chemoradiation followed by durva consolidation (PACIFIC regimen), as well as emerging data from the LAURA trial supporting Tagrisso as consolidation therapy following definitive chemoradiation in EGFR-mutant Stage III disease. The patient declined concurrent chemoradiation, citing concerns about pulmonary toxicity in the setting of her underlying COPD. After thorough discussion of risks and benefits, she elected to proceed with upfront osi monotherapy at 80 mg orally once daily, initiated approximately 6 weeks after diagnosis.

Since starting Tagrisso, she has had one interim restaging CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis with contrast at approximately 3 months, which demonstrated a meaningful partial response: the primary RUL mass decreased from 4.8 cm to 2.6 cm (approximately 46% reduction), and mediastinal lymph nodes significantly decreased in size (station 4R: 1.8 cm to 0.9 cm; station 7: 1.5 cm to 0.7 cm). No new lesions were identified at that restaging.

Today she presents for her 6-month follow-up. She reports that her shortness of breath has improved dramatically -- she is now able to walk her dog around the neighborhood, which she was unable to do at the time of diagnosis. Her initial productive cough has largely resolved; she has only occasional dry cough, which she attributes to the Tagrisso. She denies hemoptysis, pleuritic chest pain, or worsening respiratory symptoms. She reports good energy overall and has resumed some of her volunteer tutoring activities.

Regarding toxicities: she developed a grade 2 acneiform rash involving her face and upper trunk approximately 2 months into therapy. This was treated with topical clindamycin 1% gel, oral doxycycline 100 mg twice daily, and an intensive moisturizer regimen, with improvement to grade 1 within about 3 weeks; no dose reduction was required. She continues to have a grade 1 residual papular eruption on her chin and forehead, which is manageable and not distressing to her. She also notes mild paronychia of the bilateral great toes -- she has been keeping them dry and using topical antibiotic ointment. At approximately 4 months, she developed grade 1 diarrhea (2-3 loose stools per day above her baseline), managed with dietary modification and as-needed loperamide; this has largely resolved. She endorses mild dry eyes, managed with preservative-free artificial tears (Refresh Optive) applied two to three times daily.

She denies chest pain, palpitations, or syncope. She denies new headaches, vision changes, focal neurologic symptoms, or new bone pain. She denies lower extremity swelling.

She is up to date on her medications. She is adherent to Tagrisso, and states she takes it at the same time every evening with a small snack. She denies any missed doses in the past month.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Constitutional: Improved energy, no significant weight loss. No fevers or chills.
Respiratory: Dyspnea markedly improved from baseline. Occasional mild dry cough. No hemoptysis. No pleuritic pain.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or edema.
Neurologic: No headaches, no focal deficits, no vision changes, no memory concerns.
Gastrointestinal: Grade 1 diarrhea largely resolved. Good appetite. No nausea or vomiting.
Musculoskeletal: No new bone pain.
Dermatologic: Grade 1 acneiform rash (face/forehead/chin). Grade 1 paronychia, bilateral great toes.
Ophthalmologic: Grade 1 dry eye, managed with eye drops.
Endocrine: No symptoms of hyper- or hypoglycemia. Compliant with diabetes medications.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
General: Well-appearing, well-nourished Chinese-American woman. Alert and oriented x3. In no acute distress. Conversational and pleasant.
Vital Signs: Blood pressure 128/76 mmHg (left arm, seated), heart rate 72 bpm (regular), respiratory rate 16/min, oxygen saturation 96% on room air, temperature 36.8 degrees Celsius, weight [on file], height [on file].
ECOG Performance Status: 0 (from 1 at diagnosis; patient reports she is functioning at her normal baseline and above).
Head and Neck: Normocephalic. Atraumatic. Mild scattered papules on the forehead and chin with post-inflammatory erythema consistent with resolving acneiform eruption, grade 1. Sclerae anicteric. Pupils equal and reactive to light. No supraclavicular or anterior cervical lymphadenopathy. No lymphadenopathy in the neck. Oropharynx moist and without lesions.
Respiratory: Respiratory effort is unlabored. Chest is symmetrical. Dullness to percussion is absent bilaterally. On auscultation, breath sounds are clear bilaterally with very mild end-expiratory wheeze in the left base, consistent with known mild COPD; no new focal consolidation or crackles. No pleural friction rub.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. S1 and S2 present. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No jugular venous distension.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Normal bowel sounds. No hepatosplenomegaly. No palpable masses.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Bilateral great toe paronychia noted -- erythema and mild periungual swelling, non-purulent, non-fluctuant, consistent with grade 1 EGFR TKI-related paronychia. Capillary refill brisk bilaterally.
Neurologic: Alert and fully oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. No focal motor or sensory deficits. Coordination intact. Gait steady.
Skin: Papules on forehead and chin as described above. No new rash on upper trunk. No petechiae or ecchymoses.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
Complete Blood Count (CBC):
  - WBC: 5.8 x10^3/uL (normal)
  - Hemoglobin: 12.6 g/dL (mild decrease from 13.2 g/dL at baseline, stable over past 2 assessments)
  - Platelets: 214 x10^3/uL (normal)
  - Neutrophil count: 3.4 x10^3/uL (within normal limits)

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel (CMP):
  - Sodium: 138 mEq/L
  - Potassium: 4.0 mEq/L
  - Creatinine: 0.82 mg/dL (eGFR >60)
  - BUN: 16 mg/dL
  - Total bilirubin: 0.8 mg/dL
  - ALT: 28 U/L (within normal limits)
  - AST: 24 U/L (within normal limits)
  - Alkaline phosphatase: 72 U/L (normal)
  - Albumin: 3.9 g/dL

HbA1c: 6.9% (diabetes well-controlled; stable from 7.0% at prior visit)
Fasting glucose: 112 mg/dL
Magnesium: 1.9 mEq/L (lower limit of normal; will monitor)
Thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH): 2.1 mIU/L (normal)
Lipid panel: Not repeated this visit (last checked 4 months ago, within normal limits).

---

IMAGING RESULTS
CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with Contrast (performed within the past 2 weeks, reviewed today):
The right upper lobe mass has continued to decrease in size. Compared to the prior CT at 3 months (mass was 2.6 cm), the current study shows the mass now measures approximately 1.8 cm in greatest dimension -- representing roughly a 62% overall reduction from the 4.8 cm at baseline. The mass retains a small residual spiculated component but has markedly diminished in bulk. The right paratracheal lymph node at station 4R measures 0.6 cm in short axis, essentially normalizing since its prior measurement of 0.9 cm at first restaging. The subcarinal node (station 7) measures 0.5 cm, down from 0.7 cm. No new pulmonary nodules are identified. No pleural effusion. The liver, adrenal glands, and visualized bony structures are unremarkable. No new sites of concern.

Overall impression: The tumor continues to shrink on Tagrisso. The mediastinal nodes are now subcentimeter and borderline by size criteria. This is an ongoing, deepening partial response compared to both the baseline and the first restaging CT. Very encouraging trajectory.

(Note: Dedicated brain MRI is not routinely repeated at this interval given the negative baseline brain MRI and absence of neurologic symptoms; however, this will be ordered if any new neurologic symptoms develop.)

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. STAGE IIIB (T3N2M0) EGFR L858R-MUTANT NON-SMALL CELL LUNG CANCER, PULMONARY ADENOCARCINOMA -- ONGOING PARTIAL RESPONSE ON osi
Ms. Yuen is doing exceptionally well at 6 months on osi 80 mg daily. Her symptom burden has improved substantially -- dyspnea and cough largely resolved, performance status improved to ECOG 0. Restaging CT demonstrates a continued deepening partial response with the primary RUL mass now down to 1.8 cm from a baseline of 4.8 cm, and mediastinal lymph nodes now at subcentimeter size. These are very encouraging findings consistent with strong, ongoing EGFR TKI activity. We will continue osi 80 mg daily without dose modification. Next restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast will be planned at approximately 12 months (3 months from today). We will maintain low threshold to order brain MRI if any neurologic symptoms emerge.

2. EGFR TKI-RELATED DERMATOLOGIC TOXICITY -- IMPROVING
Grade 1 residual acneiform rash persists on the face. Patient is managing well with the current regimen (topical clindamycin, oral doxycycline, moisturizer). We will continue doxycycline at 100 mg twice daily for an additional 4-6 weeks with plan to taper thereafter if rash remains grade 1 or better. Paronychia of bilateral great toes: continue topical antibiotic ointment (mupirocin) and emphasize proper footwear and foot hygiene. If paronychia worsens or becomes purulent, she should contact us and may require short course of oral antibiotics. No dose reduction indicated at this time.

3. EGFR TKI-RELATED GI TOXICITY -- RESOLVED
Grade 1 diarrhea has resolved. She can continue to use loperamide as needed (she is aware of the dosing). No further intervention required. She will notify us if diarrhea recurs at grade 2 or above.

4. DRY EYE SYNDROME -- GRADE 1, MANAGED
Continue preservative-free artificial tears. If symptoms worsen, refer to ophthalmology for further evaluation.

5. TYPE 2 DIABETES MELLITUS -- WELL-CONTROLLED
HbA1c 6.9%, stable. Fasting glucose 112 mg/dL. Continue current hypoglycemic regimen. She is followed by her primary care physician for diabetes management. No changes needed at this time.

6. HYPERTENSION -- STABLE
Blood pressure 128/76 mmHg today. Continue current antihypertensive regimen (managed by PCP). No change needed from oncology standpoint.

7. MILD COPD (GOLD STAGE I) -- STABLE
Very mild end-expiratory wheeze on exam, consistent with known COPD, without significant clinical change. No active respiratory infection. She is not on bronchodilators at this time; PCP monitoring. If she develops increased wheezing or respiratory symptoms, will re-engage pulmonary.

8. MILD ANEMIA -- MONITORING
Hemoglobin 12.6 g/dL, mildly below her prior baseline. This appears stable over the past two assessments and may represent a mild chronic disease effect or mild EGFR TKI-related marrow suppression. No intervention needed at this time; will monitor CBC at next visit. Iron studies and B12/folate obtained today (results pending at time of this note); will review and address if any deficiency identified.

9. MAGNESIUM -- LOW-NORMAL
Magnesium 1.9 mEq/L -- low-normal; EGFR pathway inhibition can reduce renal magnesium reabsorption. Will advise patient to take oral magnesium oxide 400 mg daily and recheck at next visit.

FOLLOW-UP:
Return to clinic in approximately 3 months, with repeat CBC and CMP at that time. Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast to be completed approximately 1 week prior to that visit (target 9 months from Tagrisso initiation). Patient has direct nursing line phone number for urgent concerns in the interim.

Total time spent: 45 minutes. Greater than 50% of time spent on counseling and coordination of care.

Patient verbalized understanding of the plan and has no further questions at this time. She is advised to call the clinic for any new neurologic symptoms, worsening shortness of breath, or significant skin toxicities.

[Electronically signed by Attending Oncologist]
[Fellow co-signature on file]
